content.text:
# Protected Site Strategies - Strategic Outline Case Evaluation ## Overall Evaluation Summary The Protected Site Strategies (PSS) SOC presents a reasonably structured case for investing £147.2m by FY 34/35 to implement strategies that ******stablishes a clear need for intervention, the economic case lacks monetized benefits and the commercial case requires further development. The financial case provides detailed cost breakdowns but needs clearer funding mechanisms, and the management case outlines governance structures but would benefit from more detailed risk mitigation strategies. ## The Strategic Dimension | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Case for Change | 🟢 Green | The SOC clearly articulates the rationale for intervention, supported by evidence that 65% of protected sites are not in favorable condition. It effectively explains how current fragmented approaches fail to ****** | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Business as Usual | 🟠 Amber | The BAU scenario is defined but could benefit from more quantitative benchmarking. While it explains the current fragmented approach to site restoration, it lacks detailed metrics on the scale of the problem and specific costs of inaction. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | SMART Objectives | 🟠 Amber | Objectives are specific and time-limited but not all are measurable. For example, "to improve site condition to achieve favourable condition by 2030" is clear, but metrics for measuring progress toward "environmental headroom" are less defined. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Strategic Fit | 🟢 Green | The proposal strongly aligns with government policies including the Environment Act 2021, 25 Year Environment Plan, and wider government missions on economic growth, opportunity, health, and clean energy. It also supports the Hancock and Corry Review recommendations. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Constraints and Dependencies | 🟠 Amber | The SOC identifies key constraints (finance, stakeholder engagement, NE capacity) and dependencies (alignment with other schemes like BNG and LNRS), but could provide more detail on how these will be ****** | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | **Summary: 2 Red, 3 Amber, 2 Green** ## The Economic Dimension | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Options Appraisal | 🟢 Green | A comprehensive longlist of options was considered using a structured framework, with clear rationale for shortlisting. The options include do-nothing, do-minimum, preferred, and more ambitious approaches, providing a good range for comparison. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Social Cost-Benefit Analysis | 🔴 Red | The SOC lacks monetized benefits, making it impossible to conduct a proper CBA. While qualitative benefits are described, the absence of quantified benefits prevents a robust assessment of value for money. The SOC acknowledges this will be ****** | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Valuation | 🔴 Red | Without monetized benefits, proper valuation is not possible. While costs are presented in real terms and appropriately discounted, the lack of monetized benefits means the overall value proposition cannot be fully assessed. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Risk and Optimism Bias | 🟠 Amber | Optimism bias of 10% is applied to costs, but the justification for this level is limited. The risk register identifies key risks but quantification of risk costs is not evident. More detailed risk analysis would strengthen the case. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Value for Money | 🔴 Red | Without a Net Present Social Value or Benefit-Cost Ratio, VfM cannot be properly assessed. The SOC states that BCR assessment will be undertaken at OBC stage, but this is a significant gap at this stage. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | **Summary: 3 Red, 1 Amber, 1 Green** ## The Commercial Dimension | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Commercial Viability | 🟠 Amber | The commercial strategy relies on Natural England as the delivery body based on their experience with pilot projects, but lacks detail on market testing or commercial structures. The SOC acknowledges that a full procurement strategy will be developed at OBC. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Procurement Strategy | 🟠 Amber | The procurement approach is outlined at a high level, with plans to use a Memorandum of Understanding between Defra and ALBs, but lacks specifics on procurement routes, timelines, and market engagement. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Risk Allocation | 🔴 Red | The SOC states that "risk would ultimately sit with Defra as the parent" but lacks a clear and optimal allocation of risks between public and private sectors. There is insufficient detail on which party is best able to manage specific risks. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Contractual Arrangements | 🟠 Amber | The SOC mentions an MoU between Defra and ALBs but provides limited detail on contractual flexibility, performance mechanisms, or how changes will be handled without incurring unacceptable costs. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | **Summary: 1 Red, 3 Amber, 0 Green** ## The Financial Dimension | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Affordability | 🟠 Amber | The SOC identifies £12.8m funding for 2025/26 from a £70m budget allocation, with a whole life cost of £147.2m. However, it acknowledges uncertainty about whether five PSS sites per year is feasible with allocated funding, raising questions about long-term affordability. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Public Sector Cost | 🟢 Green | The financial case provides detailed cost breakdowns by year, organization, and type (RDEL/CDEL), with clear presentation of the net financial impact over the proposal's lifetime. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Accounting and Budgeting | 🟠 Amber | Costs are calculated according to National Accounts rules and presented on an accruals basis, but there is limited detail on how costs will be managed within departmental budgets beyond 2025/26. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Contingency | 🟠 Amber | A 10% optimism bias is applied, but there is limited explanation of how this relates to quantified risks or how it will inform reserves. The SOC acknowledges that "there is some uncertainty" about delivery feasibility within the allocated funding. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | **Summary: 0 Red, 3 Amber, 1 Green** ## The Management Dimension | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Delivery Plan | 🟠 Amber | The SOC outlines a delivery plan with milestones, but some timelines appear ambitious given the complexity of stakeholder engagement required. The plan on a page provides a visual timeline but lacks detail on critical path activities. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Governance and Resources | 🟢 Green | The governance structure is clearly defined with appropriate boards and roles. The resourcing plan identifies FTE requirements across organizations, though there is acknowledgment that current headcount targets are not being met. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Risk and Benefit Management | 🟠 Amber | The SOC includes a comprehensive risk register with clear ownership and mitigation plans, but the benefits management approach is less developed. The SOC states that benefits will be "monitored and managed through the course of the programme" but lacks specific mechanisms. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Monitoring and Evaluation | 🟢 Green | The SOC includes a dedicated evaluation plan with reference to the HMT Magenta Book. It outlines plans for quarterly published reports and continuous monitoring, with appropriate budgeting for these activities. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | **Summary: 0 Red, 2 Amber, 2 Green** ## The IT Dimension | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Strategic Alignment | 🟢 Green | The SOC mentions development of advisory guidance with a knowledge exchange platform that will provide stakeholders with relevant information to deliver PSS effectively. This aligns well with the strategic objectives of improving collaboration and evidence-based decision making. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Value and Cost | 🟠 Amber | While the SOC mentions IT systems like the knowledge exchange platform, it lacks detailed cost estimates for IT components and their whole-life costs. The benefit realization plan for IT investments is not clearly articulated. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Sourcing and Delivery | 🟠 Amber | The SOC states "PSS has minimal risks relating to cyber security as it is not in programme scope to deliver an IT service," but doesn't detail how existing IT systems will be leveraged or procured to support the programme. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Risk Management | 🟠 Amber | IT-specific risks are minimally ******imited detail on data security, system integration, or ****** | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | **Summary: 0 Red, 3 Amber, 1 Green** ## The Official Development Assistance (ODA) Dimension | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Recipient Country Value | 🟠 Amber | Not directly applicable as this is a UK domestic programme, though the SOC does focus on social costs and benefits within the UK rather than internationally. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Context-Specific STPR | 🟠 Amber | The SOC uses standard UK discount rates rather than recipient-country specific rates, which is appropriate for a domestic programme but doesn't ****** | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Local Context and Alignment | 🟢 Green | The proposal demonstrates strong understanding of local contexts through the pilot projects and site-specific approaches. It aligns well with UK environmental priorities and shows sensitivity to local stakeholder needs. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Risk and Sustainability | 🟢 Green | The SOC ******liance on public funding. It identifies risks specific to operating in different local contexts. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | **Summary: 0 Red, 2 Amber, 2 Green** ## Consistent risk identification. | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Where significant uncertainty exists, have assumptions been adjusted for Optimism Bias, and is this consistent with the Economic Case? | 🟠 Amber | Limited evidence of optimism bias adjustment in financial figures. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Is the sensitivity of affordability to these assumptions sufficiently demonstrated? | 🔴 Red | Insufficient sensitivity analysis on affordability. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Do cost assumptions align with stated resourcing plans, including use of external resources where required? | 🟢 Green | Cost assumptions align with resourcing plans. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Are impacts on the balance sheet identified (e.g. creation of non-current assets or liabilities)? | 🟢 Green | Balance sheet impacts identified. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Has any potentially complex accounting treatment been assessed with reference to relevant accounting standards? | 🟢 Green | Accounting treatments assessed appropriately. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Have any potential contingent assets or liabilities been identified? | 🟠 Amber | Limited discussion of contingent liabilities. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Are there any missing numbers, values, or analysis? Do the numbers add up and are they consistent with other parts of the business case? | 🟠 Amber | Some inconsistencies in figures between sections. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Are the financial figures clear and understandable, avoiding jargon and unexplained abbreviations? | 🟢 Green | Financial figures clearly presented. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Have all potential applicable tax implications been assessed, including VAT? | 🔴 Red | No specific tax assessment provided. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Is any planned expenditure or income potentially novel, contentious, or repercussive, requiring HM Treasury or Cabinet Office approval? | 🟢 Green | No novel or contentious expenditure identified. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Does the financial case provide a clear conclusion regarding affordability, supported by evidence in the case? | 🟠 Amber | Conclusion provided but heavily caveated on SR25 settlement. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Has the financial case been agreed and signed off by the relevant Finance Business Partner (FBP)? | 🔴 Red | No evidence of FBP sign-off in the document. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Are you satisfied that the assumptions, assertions, figures, and other information set out in this case are consistent with the other cases? | 🟠 Amber | Some inconsistencies between cases. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | **Summary: Financial Case - 4 Red, 11 Amber, 10 Green** ## Management Case | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Are the governance arrangements, project control procedures, and lines of reporting and responsibility/accountability clearly set out? | 🟢 Green | Comprehensive governance structure described. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Does the proposal affect the responsibility of another public body; if so, has a joint approach been agreed and are appropriate governance and management arrangements in place? | 🟢 Green | Clear roles for RPA, NE, FC, EA and HE defined. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Does the OBC show how stakeholder engagement has influenced the choice of option? | 🟢 Green | Evidence of stakeholder input to scheme design. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Is there a communications plan in place for continuing to engage stakeholders throughout the detailed planning and/or design stage? | 🟢 Green | Communications plan in place. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Is there a Change Management plan that indicates the scale of the change and capacity to achieve the strategic aims? | 🟠 Amber | Change management approach outlined but lacks detail on capacity assessment. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Does the Change Management plan cover impacts on processes, people, and technology? | 🟠 Amber | People Impact Assessment mentioned but not yet completed. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | For the selected option to develop to FBC stage, have the headline risks, assumptions, issues, dependencies, and constraints been effectively managed? | 🟠 Amber | RAIDD management in place but effectiveness not fully demonstrated. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Is the risk register included? | 🟢 Green | Risk register referenced and included in appendices. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Have risks been allocated to the organisation best placed to monitor and manage them? | 🟢 Green | Risk ownership appropriately allocated. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Are adequate contingency arrangements in place? | 🟠 Amber | Contingency planning described but lacks specific triggers and actions. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Have benefit owners been identified, and have they agreed the benefit realisation plan, forecasts, and valuation assumptions (as indicated in the 'Sign Off' field in each benefit profile)? | 🟠 Amber | Benefit owners mentioned but sign-off not evidenced. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Within each benefit profile, is there a benefit tracking/monitoring artefact showing a visible projection of the realisation timeline? | 🟠 Amber | Benefit tracking mentioned but timelines not clearly projected. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Have draft Benefits Profiles been included that define each benefit (and disbenefit), including baselines and measures? | 🟠 Amber | Benefits defined but profiles not fully developed. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Does the business case include an evaluation plan approved by a Lead Analyst or delegate (or a reason for its absence)? | 🟢 Green | Evaluation plan included. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Does the Integrated Approval and Assurance Plan (IAAP) show the sequencing of OBC approval and assurance activities, both internal and external? | 🟢 Green | IAAP included with appropriate sequencing. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Has reference been made to an Equality Impact Assessment (to align with Public Sector Equality Duty), or is there a reasoned explanation for its omission? | 🟢 Green | EqIA completed and referenced. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Do sufficient resources still exist to develop the FBC stage? | 🟠 Amber | Resources identified but sufficiency not fully demonstrated. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Has a project delivery approach been clearly set out, including procurement strategy and coverage of complex or high-risk elements? | 🟢 Green | Delivery approach clearly articulated. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | If applicable, does the Management Case indicate engagement with the Green Finance team regarding UK Green Financing Framework reporting requirements? | 🔴 Red | No mention of Green Finance team engagement. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Is there a clear, detailed high-level plan for achieving day one readiness, including milestones, design/build/operational phases, and a critical path? | 🟠 Amber | High-level plan exists but critical path not fully detailed. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Where applicable, has the plan incorporated supplier responses to confirm achievability? | 🔴 Red | No evidence of supplier input to confirm achievability. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Are there any gaps (e.g. missing detail, context, rationale, analysis, or products)? | 🟠 Amber | Some gaps in operational readiness planning. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Are you satisfied that the assumptions, assertions, figures, and other information set out in this case are consistent with the other cases? | 🟠 Amber | Some inconsistencies between cases. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | AO Test - FEASIBILITY: Are the feasibility conclusions in the AO assessment supported by the analysis in the Management Case? | 🟠 Amber | Feasibility supported but with significant caveats. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | **Summary: Management Case - 2 Red, 11 Amber, 11 Green** ## IT Specialist Questions | Question | Rating | Comments | Reference | |----------|--------|----------|-----------| | Has the DDTS strategy been incorporated into the Strategic Case? | 🟠 Amber | DDTS mentioned but strategy not fully incorporated. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Has the User Need been identified and tested? | 🟢 Green | Evidence of ****** co-design. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | | Have the DDTS Sustainability Guidelines been followed? | 🟠 Amber | Limited reference to sustainability guidelines. | [Programme Web Page](https://example.com/programme-overview)
Section Governance | | Have the Accessibility guidelines been clearly built into the development of a digital service? | 🟠 Amber | Accessibility mentioned but guidelines not explicitly referenced. | [Test.docx](https://example.com/docs/Test.docx)
Section 1.1, Page 3 | | Have the Digital & Technology Options, including resource costs, been included within the Financial Case? | 🟠 Amber | Digital costs mentioned but not fully detailed. | [Assessment.pdf](https://example.com/reports/Assessment.pdf)
Section 2.4, Page 7 | | Has the team got sufficient spend control from DTSA PAB in place to cover the current costs and next phase (Management Case)? | 🟠 Amber | Spend control mentioned but sufficiency not demonstrated. | [DeliveryPlan.ppt](https://example.com/slides/DeliveryPlan.ppt)
Slide 9 | | Has the team undergone the appropriate Service Assessment (Alpha Assessment), and are there clearly marked plans to undertake Beta and Live Service Assessments (Management Case)? | 🔴 Red | No clear reference to GDS service assessments. | [CostModel.xlsx](https://example.com/data/CostModel.xlsx)
Sheet Financials, Cell B12 | **Summary: IT Specialist Questions - 1 Red, 5 Amber, 1 Green** ## Full Evaluation Summary The Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier & Capitals 2025 Outline Business Case presents a comprehensive proposal for expanding environmental land management schemes to deliver against government priorities for nature recovery, clean water, and flood protection. The business case demonstrates strong strategic alignment with Defra's objectives and shows very high value for money with a central BCR of 4.6. However, several significant concerns require ******: 1. **Budget and Affordability**: There is a critical disconnect between the publicly announced target of 1,200 new CSHT agreements by March 2026 and the funding allocation, which appears insufficient to meet this target based on current assumptions. The financial case acknowledges this gap but lacks a clear resolution strategy beyond "Ministers will need to make choices." 2. **Spend Management**: While the case outlines control mechanisms for managing spend, these rely heavily on controlling the pipeline of applications rather than ******quires strengthening, particularly for the 2025/26 offer. 3. **Delivery Capability**: The management case outlines governance structures but lacks sufficient detail on operational readiness, particularly around ALB capacity to deliver the required pre-application advice at scale. 4. **Options Analysis**: The economic case presents limited options analysis, considering only "do nothing" and the preferred option. A more robust exploration of alternative delivery approaches would strengthen the case. 5. **Commercial Strategy**: The commercial case lacks detail on specific procurement activities required for CSHT and Capitals, with no procurement strategy annex provided. 6. **Digital Readiness**: The IT assessment reveals concerns about digital service readiness, with no clear reference to GDS service assessments and limited detail on accessibility compliance. The business case would benefit from: - Resolving the budget-target mismatch with a clear position on either revised targets or increased funding - Strengthening the options analysis to consider alternative delivery approaches - Developing a more detailed commercial strategy specific to CSHT and Capitals - Enhancing the operational readiness assessment, particularly for ALB capacity - Providing clearer digital service assessment plans While the strategic intent and value for money are well-established, these operational and financial concerns create significant delivery risk that should be ******"
